documents.title: Authorized Release Certificate — HPC-7 Hydraulic Pump Overhaul

contentJson.block7_remarks: Overhaul performed IAW CMM 73-11-01, Rev. 12. Work performed: Complete disassembly, inspection, and reassembly. Housing bore measured at 2.4985 in. (service limit 2.5010 in.) — within limits. Main shaft seal P/N 65-1234-7 replaced due to lip hardening and extrusion from heat exposure. All hardware inspected — self-locking nuts MS21044-N4 (4 ea.) replaced. Housing bolts torqued to 85 in-lbs per CMM Section 6.4. Functional pressure test performed at 3000 PSI for 5-minute hold — no leaks detected. Unit is serviceable.

documents.reasoning: Complete overhaul with functional test warrants 8130-3 release to service. All major inspection points documented with measurements.

documents.title: Major Repair — HPC-7 Hydraulic Pump Seal Replacement & Overhaul

contentJson.section5_description:
Complete overhaul of HPC-7 hydraulic pump IAW CMM 73-11-01 Rev. 12:

1. Disassembled pump housing (4 mounting bolts removed)
2. Visual inspection of housing bore — minor scoring noted, within service limits
3. Bore measurement: 2.4985 in. (limit: 2.5010 in.) — serviceable
4. Replaced main shaft seal P/N 65-1234-7 (removed seal showed lip hardening and extrusion from heat exposure)
5. Replaced self-locking nuts MS21044-N4 (4 ea.) per standard practice
6. Reassembled pump housing, torqued bolts to 85 in-lbs per CMM Section 6.4
7. Functional pressure test: 3000 PSI for 5 minutes — no leaks detected

Unit meets all return-to-service requirements per CMM 73-11-01.

documents.reasoning: Seal replacement and overhaul on a hydraulic pump qualifies as major repair requiring Form 337 documentation.

documents.title: Malfunction/Defect Report — Main Shaft Seal Heat Damage

contentJson.section3_defect_description: During overhaul inspection, main shaft seal P/N 65-1234-7 found with hardened lip and extrusion, consistent with sustained heat exposure exceeding normal operating parameters. Seal was no longer providing adequate sealing, which would have led to external hydraulic leakage if returned to service without replacement.
contentJson.section5_corrective_action: Seal replaced with new P/N 65-1234-7. Functional pressure test at 3000 PSI for 5 minutes confirmed no leaks. Root cause: likely sustained high-temperature operation. Recommend monitoring operating temperatures on this system.

documents.reasoning: Heat-damaged seal constitutes a reportable defect per FAR 21.3 — could have caused hydraulic failure if undetected.

summary: Generated three FAA compliance documents for HPC-7 hydraulic pump overhaul: 8130-3 (release to service), Form 337 (major repair documentation), and 8010-4 (defect report for heat-damaged seal). All documents cross-reference video, audio, and photo evidence. One discrepancy detected: part number transposition in 8010-4 (881700-1098 vs correct 881700-1089) — flagged for review.

discrepancies.description: Part number in 8010-4 form shows 881700-1098 but photo evidence and audio both confirm 881700-1089. Digits 8 and 9 appear transposed.
